mnemonics: MIN MAX NORM: Thread priorities range from MIN_PRIORITY (1) to MAX_PRIORITY (10), with NORM_PRIORITY (5) being the default.
keyPoints: Thread priorities are hints to the scheduler, not guarantees of execution order.
The JVM defines constants Thread.MIN_PRIORITY (1), Thread.NORM_PRIORITY (5), and Thread.MAX_PRIORITY (10).
Setting a higher priority increases a thread's chance of getting CPU time, but doesn't ensure it will run first.
commonMistakes: Relying solely on thread priorities for correct program logic, as they are platform-dependent.
Assuming a high-priority thread will always preempt a lower-priority one, which is not guaranteed by the JVM specification.
